records.ety: [OE. proselite, OF. proselite, F. proselytus, Gr. <?/, adj., that has come, n., a new comer, especially, one who has come over from heathenism to the Jewish religion; <?/ toward, to + (prob.) the root of <?/ to come.]

defs: A new convert especially a convert to some religion or religious sect, or to some particular opinion, system, or party; thus, a Gentile converted to Judaism, or a pagan converted to Christianity, is a proselyte.
quotes: Ye [Scribes and Pharisees] compass sea and land to make one proselyte.
Fresh confidence the speculatist takes From every harebrained proselyte he makes.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Proselyted (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Proselyting.]

defs: To convert to some religion, opinion, or system; to bring over.
